questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: Consider the following characteristics of a naval vessel: 1. It is India's first indigenously designed and constructed vessel of its specific class. 2. It is designed to facilitate deep sea saturation diving up to 300 meters. 3. It serves as a 'Mother Ship' for Deep Submergence Rescue Vessels (DSRV). Which of the following naval ships is best described by the characteristics given above?
questions.explanation: INS Nistar is India's first indigenously designed and constructed Diving Support Vessel. It serves as a 'Mother Ship' for the Deep Submergence Rescue Vessel (DSRV) to facilitate deep sea saturation diving up to 300m.
questions.takeaway: INS Nistar enhances the Indian Navy's search and rescue capabilities by serving as a dedicated Mother Ship for DSRV operations.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: INS Nistar: First indigenously designed and constructed Diving Support Vessel... Will serve as 'Mother Ship' for the Deep Submergence Rescue Vessel (DSRV) for deep sea saturation diving up to 300m.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: With reference to INS Arnala, consider the following statements: 1. It is India's first Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft (ASW-SWC). 2. It is the largest Indian Naval warship to be propelled by a Diesel Engine-Waterjet combination. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. INS Arnala is India's first Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft (ASW-SWC) and holds the distinction of being the largest Indian Naval warship propelled by a Diesel Engine-Waterjet combination.
questions.takeaway: INS Arnala represents a significant upgrade in India's shallow water anti-submarine warfare capabilities, utilizing specialized propulsion for its operational requirements.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: INS Arnala: India's first Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft (ASW-SWC). Largest Indian Naval warship to be propelled by a Diesel Engine-Waterjet combination.
